title: Utility Grid Strain Heat Wave Demand Spike
thesis: When regional max temperatures exceed 95°F for 3+ consecutive days AND EIA electricity demand rises above its 30-day rolling average by >2%, grid strain intensifies and utilities face margin compression. Utilities see margin compression during demand spikes without proportional rate relief; operating costs rise faster than revenue.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies and FRED electricity load series (API EIA_ELECTRICITY_DEMAND)
signal_logic: Max temp >95°F for 3 consecutive days AND EIA demand >102% of 30-day rolling mean
entry_exit: Entry: Max temp >95°F for 3 consecutive days AND EIA demand >102% of 30-day rolling mean Exit: Temperature falls below 90°F or EIA demand normalizes below 100.5% of rolling mean, or after 10 trading days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies and FRED electricity load series (API EIA_ELECTRICITY_DEMAND) via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Summer heat waves occur reliably; electricity demand data updates daily. Signal fires multiple times annually during June–September.

schema.idea: Utility Grid Strain Heat Wave Demand Spike

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies and FRED electricity load series (API EIA_ELECTRICITY_DEMAND)

schema.behavior: When regional max temperatures exceed 95°F for 3+ consecutive days AND EIA electricity demand rises above its 30-day rolling average by >2%, grid strain intensifies and utilities face margin compression.

market_impact.rationale: Utilities see margin compression during demand spikes without proportional rate relief; operating costs rise faster than revenue.

trade_logic.entry: Max temp >95°F for 3 consecutive days AND EIA demand >102% of 30-day rolling mean
trade_logic.exit: Temperature falls below 90°F or EIA demand normalizes below 100.5% of rolling mean, or after 10 trading days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Summer heat waves occur reliably; electricity demand data updates daily. Signal fires multiple times annually during June–September.

title: Freight Volume Collapse On Weak Trucking Orders
thesis: When weekly truck tonnage declines >3% week-over-week for 2 consecutive weeks, logistics providers face utilization collapse and pricing pressure. Industrial demand weakens ahead of broader slowdown. Trucking tonnage is a lead indicator of industrial production and capex demand; sustained declines signal margin compression across industrials and transportation.
data_sources: FRED series TOTALSA (total truck tonnage and carloads) via API
signal_logic: Weekly tonnage MoM decline >3% for 2 consecutive weeks; cumulative 2-week decline >6%
entry_exit: Entry: Weekly tonnage MoM decline >3% for 2 consecutive weeks; cumulative 2-week decline >6% Exit: Tonnage rebounds >1% week-over-week or 15 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series TOTALSA (total truck tonnage and carloads) via API via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight tonnage is volatile; 3% weekly swings occur 4–6 times annually. Signal fires regularly outside holiday periods.

schema.idea: Freight Volume Collapse On Weak Trucking Orders

data.source: FRED series TOTALSA (total truck tonnage and carloads) via API

schema.behavior: When weekly truck tonnage declines >3% week-over-week for 2 consecutive weeks, logistics providers face utilization collapse and pricing pressure. Industrial demand weakens ahead of broader slowdown.

market_impact.rationale: Trucking tonnage is a lead indicator of industrial production and capex demand; sustained declines signal margin compression across industrials and transportation.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly tonnage MoM decline >3% for 2 consecutive weeks; cumulative 2-week decline >6%
trade_logic.exit: Tonnage rebounds >1% week-over-week or 15 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight tonnage is volatile; 3% weekly swings occur 4–6 times annually. Signal fires regularly outside holiday periods.

title: Ev Charger Installation Slowdown Sentiment Fade
thesis: When weekly EV charger installation growth (count delta) falls >15% below its 12-week rolling average, it signals EV infrastructure investment cooling. Sentiment on clean energy capex fades. EV charger slowdown signals weakening clean tech capex and consumer EV adoption confidence, pressuring semiconductor and software exposure in energy transition.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API monthly charger count growth; cross-reference with FRED vehicle sales series
signal_logic: Weekly charger net adds fall >15% below 12-week rolling average; close below prior week close
entry_exit: Entry: Weekly charger net adds fall >15% below 12-week rolling average; close below prior week close Exit: Weekly adds rebound above 10-week rolling average or 12 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API monthly charger count growth; cross-reference with FRED vehicle sales series via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charging growth is lumpy; seasonality and policy cycles cause >15% swings 3–4 times per year. Signal matures in 2–3 weeks.

schema.idea: EV Charger Installation Slowdown Sentiment Fade

data.source: OpenChargeMap API monthly charger count growth; cross-reference with FRED vehicle sales series

schema.behavior: When weekly EV charger installation growth (count delta) falls >15% below its 12-week rolling average, it signals EV infrastructure investment cooling. Sentiment on clean energy capex fades.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger slowdown signals weakening clean tech capex and consumer EV adoption confidence, pressuring semiconductor and software exposure in energy transition.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly charger net adds fall >15% below 12-week rolling average; close below prior week close
trade_logic.exit: Weekly adds rebound above 10-week rolling average or 12 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charging growth is lumpy; seasonality and policy cycles cause >15% swings 3–4 times per year. Signal matures in 2–3 weeks.

title: Recession Whisper Google Trends Unemployment Search Surge
thesis: When combined search interest in unemployment + layoff terms spikes >40% above 52-week baseline in a single week, consumer anxiety peaks. Historical lead to risk-off rotation. Unemployment search spikes precede consumer spending weakness; discretionary retailers face demand headwinds 2–4 weeks later.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for terms: 'unemployment benefits', 'job loss', 'layoffs near me'
signal_logic: Combined normalized search score >140 (baseline 100) and >40% above 52-week mean
entry_exit: Entry: Combined normalized search score >140 (baseline 100) and >40% above 52-week mean Exit: Score falls below 120 or 20 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for terms: 'unemployment benefits', 'job loss', 'layoffs near me' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Unemployment search interest spikes 4–5 times annually during earnings seasons and macro shock periods; low barrier to fire.

schema.idea: Recession Whisper Google Trends Unemployment Search Surge

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for terms: 'unemployment benefits', 'job loss', 'layoffs near me'

schema.behavior: When combined search interest in unemployment + layoff terms spikes >40% above 52-week baseline in a single week, consumer anxiety peaks. Historical lead to risk-off rotation.

market_impact.rationale: Unemployment search spikes precede consumer spending weakness; discretionary retailers face demand headwinds 2–4 weeks later.

trade_logic.entry: Combined normalized search score >140 (baseline 100) and >40% above 52-week mean
trade_logic.exit: Score falls below 120 or 20 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Unemployment search interest spikes 4–5 times annually during earnings seasons and macro shock periods; low barrier to fire.

title: Airline Load Factor Stress Pricing Power Collapse
thesis: When weekly airline load factor (% seats filled) falls >2.5% from prior 4-week rolling average while available seat miles remain flat, pricing power collapses. Carriers cut fares and margins compress. Load factor collapse signals consumer travel demand softness and forces airlines to discount capacity, eroding margins and reducing equity valuations.
data_sources: BTS airline load factor and available seat miles via FRED/BTS adapter, weekly series
signal_logic: Weekly load factor >2.5% below 4-week rolling average AND seat miles YoY flat to +1%
entry_exit: Entry: Weekly load factor >2.5% below 4-week rolling average AND seat miles YoY flat to +1% Exit: Load factor rebounds above 4-week rolling mean or 14 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS airline load factor and available seat miles via FRED/BTS adapter, weekly series via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Airline load factors fluctuate seasonally; 2.5% swings occur 6–8 times annually. Trigger is sensitive enough to fire multiple times per quarter.

schema.idea: Airline Load Factor Stress Pricing Power Collapse

data.source: BTS airline load factor and available seat miles via FRED/BTS adapter, weekly series

schema.behavior: When weekly airline load factor (% seats filled) falls >2.5% from prior 4-week rolling average while available seat miles remain flat, pricing power collapses. Carriers cut fares and margins compress.

market_impact.rationale: Load factor collapse signals consumer travel demand softness and forces airlines to discount capacity, eroding margins and reducing equity valuations.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly load factor >2.5% below 4-week rolling average AND seat miles YoY flat to +1%
trade_logic.exit: Load factor rebounds above 4-week rolling mean or 14 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Airline load factors fluctuate seasonally; 2.5% swings occur 6–8 times annually. Trigger is sensitive enough to fire multiple times per quarter.

title: Wage Growth Demand Surge Fast Food Google Trends
thesis: When combined search interest spikes >35% above 52-week baseline, it signals labor tightness in low-wage sectors. Restaurant and hospitality operators face cost inflation; profit margins compress. Fast food wage pressure forces menu price increases; consumer traffic declines or transaction sizes stall, compressing restaurant operator margins.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'fast food hiring', 'minimum wage increase', 'restaurant worker pay'
signal_logic: Combined normalized search score >135 (baseline 100) sustained for 2 consecutive weeks
entry_exit: Entry: Combined normalized search score >135 (baseline 100) sustained for 2 consecutive weeks Exit: Score falls below 115 or 18 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'fast food hiring', 'minimum wage increase', 'restaurant worker pay' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fast food hiring/wage searches spike seasonally (spring/summer) and during policy announcements; fires 5–7 times per year.

schema.idea: Wage Growth Demand Surge Fast Food Google Trends

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'fast food hiring', 'minimum wage increase', 'restaurant worker pay'

schema.behavior: When combined search interest spikes >35% above 52-week baseline, it signals labor tightness in low-wage sectors. Restaurant and hospitality operators face cost inflation; profit margins compress.

market_impact.rationale: Fast food wage pressure forces menu price increases; consumer traffic declines or transaction sizes stall, compressing restaurant operator margins.

trade_logic.entry: Combined normalized search score >135 (baseline 100) sustained for 2 consecutive weeks
trade_logic.exit: Score falls below 115 or 18 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fast food hiring/wage searches spike seasonally (spring/summer) and during policy announcements; fires 5–7 times per year.

title: Commodity Input Cost Shock Mining Sector Margin Crunch
thesis: When FRED commodity index (broad basket) spikes >3% in a single day while XLB stays flat or declines, input costs surge faster than mining/materials producer pricing. Margin squeeze imminent. Materials producers cannot immediately pass through input cost shocks; operating margins compress until prices normalize or they cut production.
data_sources: FRED commodity price indices (energy, metals, agricultural) daily series; XLB ETF daily price via price_only adapter
signal_logic: Commodity index +3% day AND XLB +0.5% or lower same day
entry_exit: Entry: Commodity index +3% day AND XLB +0.5% or lower same day Exit: XLB closes >1.5% above entry close or 8 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED commodity price indices (energy, metals, agricultural) daily series; XLB ETF daily price via price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily commodity shocks >3% occur 2–3 times per month. Divergence with materials ETF pricing happens 4–6 times annually.

schema.idea: Commodity Input Cost Shock Mining Sector Margin Crunch

data.source: FRED commodity price indices (energy, metals, agricultural) daily series; XLB ETF daily price via price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When FRED commodity index (broad basket) spikes >3% in a single day while XLB stays flat or declines, input costs surge faster than mining/materials producer pricing. Margin squeeze imminent.

market_impact.rationale: Materials producers cannot immediately pass through input cost shocks; operating margins compress until prices normalize or they cut production.

trade_logic.entry: Commodity index +3% day AND XLB +0.5% or lower same day
trade_logic.exit: XLB closes >1.5% above entry close or 8 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily commodity shocks >3% occur 2–3 times per month. Divergence with materials ETF pricing happens 4–6 times annually.

title: Real Estate Cap Rate Compression Yield Chase Unwind
thesis: When 10-year mortgage rates fall >15 bps in a week but XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5%, yield-chasing capital rotates away from real estate. Sentiment on cap rate compression fades. Rate cuts typically boost REIT valuations; when they don't, it signals investor skepticism about fundamental demand or dividend sustainability.
data_sources: FRED commercial real estate cap rate series (MORTGAGE30US, TERMCBCCALLNS) daily; XLRE ETF daily price via price_only adapter
signal_logic: 10-year mortgage rate falls >15 bps weekly AND XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5% over same period
entry_exit: Entry: 10-year mortgage rate falls >15 bps weekly AND XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5% over same period Exit: XLRE outperforms SPY >1% or 12 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED commercial real estate cap rate series (MORTGAGE30US, TERMCBCCALLNS) daily; XLRE ETF daily price via price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage rates are volatile; 15 bps weekly moves occur 8–10 times annually. Signal matures quickly and fires 3–4 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Real Estate Cap Rate Compression Yield Chase Unwind

data.source: FRED commercial real estate cap rate series (MORTGAGE30US, TERMCBCCALLNS) daily; XLRE ETF daily price via price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When 10-year mortgage rates fall >15 bps in a week but XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5%, yield-chasing capital rotates away from real estate. Sentiment on cap rate compression fades.

market_impact.rationale: Rate cuts typically boost REIT valuations; when they don't, it signals investor skepticism about fundamental demand or dividend sustainability.

trade_logic.entry: 10-year mortgage rate falls >15 bps weekly AND XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5% over same period
trade_logic.exit: XLRE outperforms SPY >1% or 12 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage rates are volatile; 15 bps weekly moves occur 8–10 times annually. Signal matures quickly and fires 3–4 times per quarter.

title: Bank Deposit Outflow Fear Inversion Signal
thesis: When weekly bank deposit flows turn negative (outflows exceed inflows) for 2 consecutive weeks AND high-yield savings rates spike >0.5% above prior month, retail depositors flee traditional banks. Bank funding stress accelerates. Deposit outflows force banks to raise rates, compress NIM, and rely on wholesale funding. Equity valuations contract on net interest margin compression.
data_sources: FRED series on bank deposits (BMNRNJ) and high-yield savings rate (MMNRNJ) weekly; XLF ETF daily price via price_only adapter
signal_logic: Weekly deposit growth <0% for 2 consecutive weeks AND HY savings rate spike >0.5% MoM
entry_exit: Entry: Weekly deposit growth <0% for 2 consecutive weeks AND HY savings rate spike >0.5% MoM Exit: Deposit growth returns >0.5% or 15 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series on bank deposits (BMNRNJ) and high-yield savings rate (MMNRNJ) weekly; XLF ETF daily price via price_only adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Deposit volatility and HY rate spreads move 4–6 times per year; signal fires reliably during rate hike cycles.

schema.idea: Bank Deposit Outflow Fear Inversion Signal

data.source: FRED series on bank deposits (BMNRNJ) and high-yield savings rate (MMNRNJ) weekly; XLF ETF daily price via price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When weekly bank deposit flows turn negative (outflows exceed inflows) for 2 consecutive weeks AND high-yield savings rates spike >0.5% above prior month, retail depositors flee traditional banks. Bank funding stress accelerates.

market_impact.rationale: Deposit outflows force banks to raise rates, compress NIM, and rely on wholesale funding. Equity valuations contract on net interest margin compression.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly deposit growth <0% for 2 consecutive weeks AND HY savings rate spike >0.5% MoM
trade_logic.exit: Deposit growth returns >0.5% or 15 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Deposit volatility and HY rate spreads move 4–6 times per year; signal fires reliably during rate hike cycles.

title: Weather-driven Agricultural Futures Shock Consumer Food Inflation
thesis: When grain belt precipitation falls >50% below normal for 14+ consecutive days AND FRED agricultural commodity indices spike >2.5% in a single week, food input costs surge. Consumer staples pricing power tested. Food inflation pressures staples consumers; if staples raise prices too much, volume declines. If they absorb costs, margins compress. Either way, equity returns suffer.
data_sources: Open-Meteo precipitation and temperature daily data for US grain belt; cross-reference FRED agricultural commodity prices weekly
signal_logic: Grain belt precip <50% of 30-year normal for 14+ days AND ag commodity index +2.5% weekly
entry_exit: Entry: Grain belt precip <50% of 30-year normal for 14+ days AND ag commodity index +2.5% weekly Exit: Precip rebounds to >70% normal or commodity index declines >1% or 20 trading days elapsed
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo precipitation and temperature daily data for US grain belt; cross-reference FRED agricultural commodity prices weekly via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal dry spells and commodity spikes occur predictably; signal fires 3–5 times per year, especially during growing season (May–August).

schema.idea: Weather-Driven Agricultural Futures Shock Consumer Food Inflation

data.source: Open-Meteo precipitation and temperature daily data for US grain belt; cross-reference FRED agricultural commodity prices weekly

schema.behavior: When grain belt precipitation falls >50% below normal for 14+ consecutive days AND FRED agricultural commodity indices spike >2.5% in a single week, food input costs surge. Consumer staples pricing power tested.

market_impact.rationale: Food inflation pressures staples consumers; if staples raise prices too much, volume declines. If they absorb costs, margins compress. Either way, equity returns suffer.

trade_logic.entry: Grain belt precip <50% of 30-year normal for 14+ days AND ag commodity index +2.5% weekly
trade_logic.exit: Precip rebounds to >70% normal or commodity index declines >1% or 20 trading days elapsed

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal dry spells and commodity spikes occur predictably; signal fires 3–5 times per year, especially during growing season (May–August).
